title: Cipher Mining Inc (CIFR) Q1 2024 Earnings Report
text: Cipher Mining demonstrated strong financial performance in Q1 2024. The company achieved revenue increased from $43 million to $48 million quarter over quarter, while GAAP net earnings surged to $40 million compared to $11 million previously. Self-mining capacity reached 7.7 exahash per second. Bitcoin inventory stood at 2,033 coins. Cash reserves totaled $96 million. Mined 924 Bitcoin generating $48.1 million in quarterly revenue. All-in electricity cost per bitcoin: $11,912. CEO Tyler Page outlined expansion plans, including a full 300-megawatt build-out at the Black Pearl site with projected costs around $420 million. The company plans financing through operational cash flow, Bitcoin holdings, and potentially tapping into equity or debt markets. Negative factors include the bitcoin halving's impact on block rewards and intense industry competition. The company acknowledged a significant squeeze on minor economics post-halving, though positioned itself favorably as a low-cost producer with opportunities for strategic acquisitions.
